items.title: SpudCell: The first synthetic cell with a complete cell cycle
items.summary: Researchers have created SpudCell, the first synthetic organism with a complete, functioning cell cycle. This breakthrough in synthetic biology opens new frontiers for biotech applications in regenerative medicine, agriculture, and manufacturing.

items.title: Restaurants can now accept orders placed directly from ChatGPT and Claude thanks to Square's new, low-fee, no setup integration
items.summary: Square integrated direct ordering from ChatGPT and Claude into restaurant workflows with low friction and minimal fees. This partnership demonstrates how AI assistants are becoming distribution channels for commerce, allowing restaurants to reach order-placement preferences without building proprietary integrations.

items.title: Sony Deletes 551 Movies PlayStation Owners Paid For
items.summary: Sony removed 551 films from PlayStation digital storefronts, affecting customers who purchased licenses. This incident highlights the ongoing tension between licensing rights, consumer ownership, and digital asset permanence in streaming ecosystems.

items.title: Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
items.summary: Venice AI secured $65M in Series A funding and achieved unicorn status with its privacy-focused AI platform. The round signals investor confidence in differentiated AI approaches emphasizing data protection as a competitive moat in an increasingly crowded market.

items.title: For First Time, a Cell Built from Scratch Grows and Divides
items.summary: Scientists have created a fully synthetic cell capable of growth and division, representing a major breakthrough in synthetic biology. This achievement opens new investment pathways in biotech, pharmaceuticals, and cellular engineering, though commercialization timelines remain uncertain.

items.title: Gemini Spark, Google’s agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
items.summary: Google expanded Gemini Spark's availability to macOS, extending its agentic AI assistant to a broader user base. This move reflects the acceleration of AI agent deployment across consumer platforms as major tech companies compete for usage leadership.

items.title: Sony to end physical PlayStation game disc production in 2028
items.summary: Sony announced the discontinuation of physical PlayStation game disc manufacturing by 2028, signaling the industry's accelerated shift toward digital distribution. The decision reflects broader consumer behavior trends and has implications for game publisher economics and supply chain strategy.

items.title: Figma Grows 46% and Trades at 6x. Everyone Else Growing 30%+ Trades at 15-36x.
items.summary: Figma commands a significant valuation premium despite slower growth relative to 30%+ growth SaaS peers, indicating the market rewards product-market fit and user loyalty over raw growth rates. This suggests venture returns may depend less on hypergrowth and more on durable competitive advantages and customer stickiness.

items.title: This Cell Feeds, Grows and Reproduces. and It's Manmade
items.summary: Researchers demonstrated a laboratory-engineered cell with autonomous feeding, growth, and reproduction capabilities, advancing synthetic biology toward practical applications. This development could accelerate venture interest in cellular engineering startups and biotech platforms targeting disease modeling and drug production.
